text: Maximal rates of fat oxidation are reached at exercise intensities between 59% and 64% of VO2max in trained individuals.
Maximal rates of fat oxidation are reached at exercise intensities between 47% and 52% of VO2max in the general population.
Fat oxidation rates increase from low to moderate exercise intensities and then decrease at high intensities.
Fat oxidation is higher during running than cycling at comparable intensities.
Endurance training induces adaptations that result in increased fat oxidation capacity.
Ingestion of carbohydrate in the hours before or at the start of exercise significantly reduces fat oxidation compared with fasted conditions.
Fasting longer than 6 hours before exercise optimizes fat oxidation rates.
